message:
fix: improve SSH key generation UX with clearer instructions

- Add explanation of where SSH keys are stored (~/.ssh folder)
- Show OS-specific paths (Mac vs Windows)
- Clarify the 3 prompts users will see (file location, passphrase x2)
- Explain ~ symbol and hidden folder convention

Addresses UX audit finding about users not knowing where keys are stored.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -85,6 +85,29 @@ export default function GenerateSSHKeyPage() { Think of it like a lock and key: you share the lock, but only you have the key. + {/* ~/.ssh folder explanation */} + +

+ SSH keys are stored in a special folder called ~/.ssh on your computer. +

+
    +
  • + On {os === "mac" ? "Mac" : "Windows"}:{" "} + {os === "mac" ? ( + /Users/yourname/.ssh/ + ) : ( + C:\\Users\\yourname\\.ssh\\ + )} +
    • +
  • + The ~ symbol is shorthand for your home folder. +
    • +
  • + The . prefix makes it a hidden folder (that's normal for config files). +
    • +
+ + {/* Privacy assurance */}
@@ -114,9 +137,15 @@ export default function GenerateSSHKeyPage() {

Step 1: Generate the key

- Run this command in your terminal. Press Enter twice - when asked for a passphrase (leave it empty for now). + Run this command in your terminal. You'll be asked 3 questions—here's how to answer:

+
+
+

1. File location: Press Enter to accept the default

+

2. Passphrase: Press Enter (leave empty)

+

3. Confirm passphrase: Press Enter again

+
+
